channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Transportation Secretary Duffy filmed a reality show, funded by firms he regulates
item.description: Secretary Duffy, his wife and nine kids took a "Great American Road Trip" to celebrate America's 250th. They say no taxpayers funded the project. But a nonprofit with transport-related sponsors did.
item.content:encoded: Secretary Duffy, his wife and nine kids took a "Great American Road Trip" to celebrate America's 250th. They say no taxpayers funded the project. But a nonprofit with transport-related sponsors did.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: He's just turned 100, and now you're invited on 'Attenborough's Greatest Adventure'
item.description: David Attenborough's acclaimed nature series Life on Earth began production 50 years ago. Now, a PBS documentary captures the host looking back on that series as it's projected in a screening room.
item.content:encoded: David Attenborough's acclaimed nature series Life on Earth began production 50 years ago. Now, a PBS documentary captures the host looking back on that series as it's projected in a screening room.
item.dc:creator: David Bianculli

item.title: She spent a year using AI to do almost everything. Here's what she learned
item.description: Tech writer Joanna Stern used AI to read medical results, respond to texts and serve as her therapist. She says her emotional connection to it was unsettling. Her new book is I Am Not a Robot.
item.content:encoded: Tech writer Joanna Stern used AI to read medical results, respond to texts and serve as her therapist. She says her emotional connection to it was unsettling. Her new book is I Am Not a Robot.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: In 'The Young Will Remember,' a Korean War reporter gets stranded behind enemy lines
item.description: In Eve J. Chung's new novel, Ellie Chang ends up stuck in a place she's only known as enemy territory, reliant on strangers to help her get home.
item.content:encoded: In Eve J. Chung's new novel, Ellie Chang ends up stuck in a place she's only known as enemy territory, reliant on strangers to help her get home.
item.dc:creator: Dhanika Pineda

item.title: Study finds engaging with the arts can slow biological aging
item.description: A new study finds that enjoyment of arts and culture slows down biological aging.
item.content:encoded: A new study finds that enjoyment of arts and culture slows down biological aging.
item.dc:creator: Allison Aubrey

item.title: Stephen Colbert and late night hosts strike again as his show nears finale
item.description: Stephen Colbert invited his "best television friends," fellow late night hosts John Oliver, Seth Meyers and the two Jimmies— Kimmel and Fallon— to join him, as his final show on CBS is set for May 21.
item.content:encoded:
Stephen Colbert invited his "best television friends," fellow late night hosts John Oliver, Seth Meyers and the two Jimmies— Kimmel and Fallon— to join him, as his final show on CBS is set for May 21.

(Image credit: Scott Kowalchyk)
item.dc:creator: Mandalit del Barco

item.title: Author Anne Lamott shares how she had to unlearn what others thought of her
item.description: On Wild Card, well-known guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Author Anne Lamott opens up about how she had to unlearn what others thought of her.
item.content:encoded: On Wild Card, well-known guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Author Anne Lamott opens up about how she had to unlearn what others thought of her.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Martin

item.title: Baseball bards from across the country gather to celebrate the sport with poetry
item.description: America's national pastime is celebrated at a minor league park in Massachusetts... with poetry
item.content:encoded: America's national pastime is celebrated at a minor league park in Massachusetts... with poetry
item.dc:creator: Andrea Shea

item.title: Zach Galifianakis cultivates plenty of laughs in 'This Is a Gardening Show'
item.description: In the course of his delightful Netflix series, Galifianakis learns how to graft apple trees, make richer compost and generally self-sustain. "The future is agrarian," he says in every episode.
item.content:encoded: In the course of his delightful Netflix series, Galifianakis learns how to graft apple trees, make richer compost and generally self-sustain. "The future is agrarian," he says in every episode.
item.dc:creator: David Bianculli

item.title: Will Sharpe imagines Mozart's day-to-day in 'Amadeus'
item.description: Sharpe played a newly rich tech bro on vacation in The White Lotus. Now he's starring as Mozart, a musical genius who struggles to "read the room" in a new limited TV series.
item.content:encoded:
Sharpe played a newly rich tech bro on vacation in The White Lotus. Now he's starring as Mozart, a musical genius who struggles to "read the room" in a new limited TV series.

(Image credit: Adrienn Szabo)
item.dc:creator: Ann Marie Baldonado
